sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports pain in the right back, initially attributed to moving hay but later confirmed as a kidney stone. He also reports nausea and sweating related to the stone. He denies seeing blood in his urine.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation in the right abdomen, particularly over the kidney. Vital signs are stable; blood pressure and heart rate are within normal limits. Neurological exam is unremarkable.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a confirmed kidney stone, likely causing the reported pain and associated symptoms.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The doctor ordered a urine culture and BMP. The doctor prescribed oxycodone for pain relief and Tylenol for breakthrough pain. The doctor scheduled a follow-up appointment in one to two weeks to monitor progress and discuss potential lithotripsy if the stone has not passed.
